# Probability Disclosure Enforcement in Korea (Enforcement and Case Law)

## Scope and As-of Date
- Jurisdictions: KR
- As-of date: 2026-05-07
- Assumptions: probability disclosure cases as of 2026-05; pre-appellate state assumed
- Exclusions: civil class actions, foreign-jurisdiction enforcement

## Conclusion Summary
- Active KR enforcement establishes precedent for probability-disclosure misrepresentation [src_002] applying [src_001].

## Enforcement Timeline
| Date | Authority | Action | Legal Basis | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|-------------|--------|
| 2024-Q3 | KFTC | Corrective order against probability misrepresentation | Article 33-2 [src_001] | [src_002] |

## Case and Decision Summaries
- Case name: KFTC Decision 2024-XXX
- Holding: Probability misrepresentation in randomized item sales is a deceptive practice subject to corrective order
- Practical implication: Sellers must align disclosed probabilities with actual drop rates and retain logs
- Pinpoint: [src_002] operative paragraphs

## Counter-Analysis
Per `knowledge/output-modes/counter-analysis-checklist.md`. The single
KFTC decision in [src_002] has no appellate review yet; lower-vs-higher
court split is theoretically open. Risk level Medium.

## Practical Implications
- Enforcement landscape: KFTC active; precedent value of [src_002] not yet appellate-tested
- Compliance risk: high for operators with imprecise probability claims
- Monitoring points: any appellate ruling narrowing [src_002]; second-instance KFTC decisions

## Annotated Bibliography
- Primary: [src_001] Korean Game Industry Promotion Act probability disclosure provisions, Article 33-2; [src_002] KFTC Decision 2024-XXX
- Secondary: none cited in this deliverable

- Statutory basis -> [src_001] Article 33-2
- Enforcement precedent -> [src_002] operative paragraphs
